sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) Price Target Lowered to $150.00 at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group lowered its price target for Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) to $150.00 from $161.00 but maintained a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 47.10%. This adjustment follows Wynn missing its quarterly EPS consensus despite slightly beating revenue estimates. The casino operator currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with an average target price of $138.53.

feed.title: Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) Earns Buy Rating from Needham & Company LLC
feed.summary: Needham & Company LLC has reiterated a "Buy" rating for Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) with a $255 price target, suggesting a 57% upside. This follows Broadridge's strong quarterly performance, exceeding EPS and revenue estimates, and setting optimistic FY2026 guidance. Despite a mixed analyst view, the CEO recently increased his stake, indicating confidence in the company's future.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR) Price Target Raised to $26.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has raised its price target for Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR) from $24.00 to $26.00, maintaining a "hold" rating despite observed financial challenges such as missed earnings estimates, negative margins, and a high debt-to-equity ratio. Other analysts, including Morgan Stanley and Susquehanna, have also adjusted their price targets, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target of $32.00. The stock recently traded at $26.58, with a market capitalization of $5.41 billion.

feed.title: Celebrate Earth Month with Kroger and Round Up at the Register for World Wildlife Fund
feed.summary: The Kroger Co. is inviting customers to celebrate Earth Month by rounding up their purchases at checkout to support the World Wildlife Fund's (WWF) conservation efforts. From April 1st to April 30th, customers can donate, and Kroger will match donations up to $50,000 to advance WWF's sustainability initiatives. This initiative is part of Kroger's Zero Hunger | Zero Waste mission, reinforcing their commitment to community health and environmental stewardship.

feed.title: General Mills plant in St. Charles to close in June
feed.summary: General Mills announced the closure of its plant in St. Charles, Missouri, effective early June, leading to 163 job eliminations. This decision follows earlier plant closure announcements in October 2025. Employees affected by this closure are not eligible for transfer or displacement to other facilities, and the closure will be permanent.

feed.title: Science Will Win season finale explores AI in cancer care with Pfizer Chief Oncology Officer Jeff Legos
feed.summary: The sixth season finale of Pfizer's "Science Will Win" podcast, which has reached 3 million downloads, focuses on the role of artificial intelligence (AI) in cancer care and research. Hosted by Dr. Raven Baxter, the episode features Pfizer Chief Oncology Officer Jeff Legos and other experts discussing how AI and digital technologies are being integrated to accelerate oncology advances and connect scientific breakthroughs with patient care. The season has explored various aspects of oncology, from early-onset cancer to survivorship and the contributions of women in the field.

feed.title: Netflix launches new gaming app for kids called Netflix Playground
feed.summary: Netflix is expanding its gaming features with a new kid-friendly app called Netflix Playground, targeting children 8 years old and younger. The app will include popular titles like "Playtime With Peppa Pig" and "Sesame Street," and all games will be playable offline with no ads, in-app purchases, or extra fees. This launch follows Netflix's recent price increase and its ongoing investment in gaming since 2021, aiming to provide a safe and engaging space for children.

feed.title: The Mosaic Company stock: What you should know now as a smart investor
feed.summary: The Mosaic Company is a leading global producer of phosphate and potash fertilizers, essential for food production. The article details Mosaic's business model, market position, and industry drivers, highlighting its role in global food security and its vertical integration. It also covers analyst views, potential risks, and recommendations for investors considering adding MOS stock to their portfolios.

feed.title: Chlorine taste should fade as parts of New Jersey switch back
feed.summary: New Jersey American Water announced it will resume using chloramines at its Raritan-Millstone and Canal Road treatment plants the week of April 20, 2026. This follows a temporary ten-week switch to chlorine for routine maintenance, during which some customers may have noticed a chlorine taste or smell. The company has used chloramines for water disinfection since the 1970s, which meet all EPA and NJDEP drinking water standards.

feed.title: KFC turns a value-meal campaign into a Spotify single and LA street drop
feed.summary: KFC has launched a new marketing campaign centered around a Spotify single titled "Finger Lickin' Machine" to promote its new Box Feasts, priced at $7, $9, and $11. The campaign includes a 90-second music video-style spot with choreography by Rich + Tone Talauega and limited-edition physical single drops in Los Angeles. This initiative aims to engage consumers across various platforms, from streaming to in-person experiences, moving beyond traditional fast-food advertising.

feed.title: RJF: Jefferies Lowers Price Target for Raymond James Financial
feed.summary: Jefferies has lowered its price target for Raymond James Financial (RJF) from $165.00 to $145.00, while maintaining a 'Hold' rating. This adjustment reflects a cautious market sentiment despite the firm's diversified financial services, which are largely driven by its wealth management franchise in the United States. Wall Street analysts currently forecast an average price target of $183.10 for RJF, implying a potential upside of 28.56% from its current price.

feed.title: Zoox Rolls into L.A. with Robotaxi Test Run
feed.summary: Zoox Inc. is partnering with Uber Technologies Inc. to integrate its autonomous vehicles into Uber’s ride-hailing app, with Los Angeles serving as the initial test market by mid-2027. These purpose-built robotaxis, an independent subsidiary of Amazon.com Inc., have been quietly mapping L.A. for a year and are designed for a unique passenger experience with no steering wheel or pedals. The company is strategically rolling out its services city by city, mapping unique driving conditions to ensure reliability and safety.

feed.title: Calls of the Day: Royal Caribbean, Las Vegas Sands, Wynn Resorts and Netflix
feed.summary: This article highlights the "Calls of the Day" debated by The Investment Committee, focusing on companies like Royal Caribbean, Las Vegas Sands, Wynn Resorts, and Netflix. It appears to be a segment from a financial news program, likely discussing analyst ratings or investment recommendations for these specific stocks. The primary content is a video segment capturing this discussion.

feed.title: Pentagon to Request $4.5 Billion for Tomahawk Missiles in 2027
feed.summary: The Pentagon plans to request $4.5 billion for Tomahawk missiles in its fiscal 2027 budget, earmarking $3 billion for 1,512 new missiles from RTX Corp and an additional $1.5 billion for modifications to existing Navy weapons. This spending is driven by the depletion of inventories due to the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran. The request is part of an unprecedented $1.5 trillion national defense budget.

feed.title: Bank of America sells Invesco Advantage (VKI) shares worth $29,156
feed.summary: Bank of America CORP /DE/, along with Merrill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith INC, reported selling 3,359 shares of Invesco Advantage Municipal Income Trust II (VKI) common stock on April 2, 2026. The sale, valued at $29,156, occurred at a price of $8.68 per share. On the same day, the reporting owner also acquired an identical number of shares at the same price, totaling $29,156.

feed.title: Ford recalls nearly 255K SUVs for camera image processing module errors
feed.summary: Ford is recalling nearly 255,000 SUVs, including the 2025 Ford Explorer and Lincoln Aviator, due to a software flaw in the camera image processing module. This defect can lead to an intermittent loss of the rearview camera image or advanced driver-assist features like blindspot monitoring and lane-keeping assist, increasing the risk of a crash. The issue is more prevalent in dense urban environments and will be addressed with an over-the-air software update or a free dealer visit.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: What to Expect From General Motors' Report
feed.summary: General Motors Company (GM) is set to release its Q1 fiscal 2026 earnings before the market opens on April 28. Analysts anticipate an EPS of $2.64, a 5% decrease from the previous year, despite GM consistently beating earnings estimates in the last four quarters. The stock has seen significant growth over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500 and the consumer discretionary sector, and Wall Street maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price indicating a potential 26.1% upside.

feed.title: Bank of America (NYSE: BAC) unit matches VKI share buy and sale
feed.summary: Bank of America Corporation and its subsidiary Merrill Lynch reported same-day open-market trades of Invesco Advantage Municipal Income Trust II (VKI) common stock. On April 2, 2026, an entity they report acquired and then sold 3,359 shares at $8.68 per share, resulting in zero shares indirectly held from these transactions. The reporting parties disclaim beneficial ownership except for any pecuniary interest and state that any profit recoverable under Section 16(b) will be remitted to the issuer.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Lowers Price Target on TTWO, Maintains Overweight Ra
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has lowered its price target for Take-Two Interactive (TTWO) from $295.00 to $293.00, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This adjustment comes amidst varied assessments from other financial analysts for the video game developer and publisher. Take-Two, known for major franchises like Grand Theft Auto and NBA 2K, sees a significant portion of its sales from in-game spending and mobile games.

feed.title: Analysts Have Conflicting Sentiments on These Technology Companies: Paylocity (PCTY) and Corning (GLW)
feed.summary: Analysts are reporting conflicting sentiments on technology companies Paylocity (PCTY) and Corning (GLW). UBS maintained a Hold rating on Paylocity with a $120.00 price target, while the consensus is a Strong Buy at $167.81. For Corning, Mizuho Securities maintained a Buy rating with a $155.00 price target, aligning with a Moderate Buy consensus.

feed.title: Schwab Trading Activity Index™: STAX Score Sees Steepest Monthly Drop Since May 2025
feed.summary: The Schwab Trading Activity Index™ (STAX) fell to 56.04 in March, marking its steepest monthly decline since May 2025, primarily due to the start of war in Iran. Schwab clients showed a shift towards more diversified exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and away from individual stock picking amidst increased market volatility. This decline, the first since December, was accompanied by a retreat in long-term bullish sentiment across various investor age groups, including Gen-X, Gen-Z, Millennials, and Baby Boomers.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Invites FFA Members Nationwide to Highlight “Life Out Here” in Harvest Fest Tote Bag Design Competition
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company has launched a contest for FFA members nationwide to design the official 2026 Harvest Fest tote bag. The winning design will be featured during Tractor Supply’s August 29th Harvest Fest event, and the winner will receive gift cards for themselves and their FFA chapter. This initiative reinforces Tractor Supply's long-standing partnership with FFA, supporting youth involvement in agriculture.

feed.title: 5 Reasons the Invesco QQQ ETF Could Be Headed for a Triple-Digit Rally
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ ETF, which tracks the NASDAQ Composite Index, is poised for a significant rally, potentially adding triple-digits to portfolios over time, driven by strong technical signals, robust earnings outlooks—especially from big tech and AI companies like NVIDIA—and attractive valuations. Institutions are actively buying into these leading tech stocks, further underpinning the market's upside potential. Outperformance by the NASDAQ Composite against consensus estimates in Q2 2026, largely due to underestimated AI demand, is expected to be a key catalyst, despite potential risks from elevated oil prices.

feed.title: FFA students can win $1,250 in Tractor Supply Harvest Fest contest
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company has launched a design contest exclusively for FFA members to create the official 2026 Harvest Fest tote bag. The winning student will receive a $250 Tractor Supply gift card, and their FFA chapter will get a $1,000 gift card. The contest aims to highlight the "Life Out Here" theme and support the next generation of agricultural leaders.

feed.title: Sally Beauty Appoints Adrianne Lee as Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: Sally Beauty Holdings, Inc. has appointed Adrianne Lee as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective April 28, 2026. Lee replaces Marlo Cormier and brings extensive experience from Bed Bath & Beyond, The Hertz Corporation, and other finance-focused roles. She expressed enthusiasm for contributing to Sally Beauty's growth strategy and shareholder value.
feed.source: Happi | Household And Personal Products Industry
feed.source_domain: Happi | Household And Personal Products Industry

feed.title: Svante & MLTC partnership agrees to sell Microsoft 626,000 tonnes of carbon removal credits
feed.summary: A partnership between Svante and MLTC has reportedly secured an agreement to sell Microsoft 626,000 tonnes of carbon removal credits. The article, originating from Reuters, was published on April 6, 2026. This content is reserved for members on MarketScreener, indicating it is likely a premium news item.

feed.title: Suncorp preferred over IAG on pricing growth, Morgan Stanley says
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analysts favor Suncorp Group over Insurance Australia Group due to stronger top-line growth and re-rating potential. A March 2026 premium survey revealed home insurance pricing rose 6% year-over-year, while motor insurance lagged at 1% due to Allianz cutting prices. Suncorp notably increased motor prices, and home insurance showed strong momentum with new premiums up 6% in Q1 2026, leading Morgan Stanley to forecast higher organic gross written premium growth for Suncorp Consumer.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley cuts U.S. airline estimates across the board on jet fuel costs
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has downgraded its estimates for the entire U.S. airline sector due to the impact of surging jet fuel prices, exacerbated by the Middle East conflict. Despite these challenges, the brokerage noted the resilience of airlines in early 2026, driven by strong travel demand and booking trends, which could lead to a "new era of earnings resilience" if momentum continues. However, the analysts predict challenges through 2026, with potential recovery delays to 2027 if demand weakens or fuel prices remain high.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights NVIDIA, AMD, Applied Materials, Lam Research and Micron Technology
feed.summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights NVIDIA, AMD, Applied Materials, Lam Research, and Micron Technology, focusing on two "pick-and-shovel" AI stocks, Applied Materials and Lam Research, poised for growth. Despite a recent decline in NVIDIA's shares, Applied Materials and Lam Research showed significant increases in Q1 2026, driven by rising AI investments and strong demand for semiconductor equipment. The article details their financial performance, growth prospects, and strong Zacks Ranks, suggesting they could emulate NVIDIA's success.

feed.title: $10,000 to Invest? Coca-Cola or Johnson & Johnson- One Stock Is More Reliable
feed.summary: This article compares Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) and Coca-Cola (KO) as investment options, highlighting their recent Q4 earnings, growth strategies, and dividend reliability. While both companies have a 63-year dividend streak, J&J is focused on biotech pipeline expansion and aggressive portfolio reshaping, demonstrating stronger near-term growth in oncology. Coca-Cola, conversely, is moving towards an asset-light model, relying on brand power and pricing discipline, but faces challenges with volume declines and impairment charges.

feed.title: eBay Inc Stock (EBAY) Moved Up by 3.63% on Apr 6: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) stock increased by 3.63% on April 6, driven by strong Q4 2025 results and positive Q1 2026 guidance. Strategic platform enhancements and the anticipated acquisition of Depop also contributed to investor confidence. Despite a Wells Fargo & Company price target reduction, overall analyst sentiment and technical indicators remain largely positive for EBAY.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights NVIDIA, AMD, Applied Materials, Lam Research and Micron Technology
feed.summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog features NVIDIA, AMD, Applied Materials, Lam Research, and Micron Technology, focusing on "pick-and-shovel" AI stocks poised for growth. It highlights Applied Materials and Lam Research as strong contenders, capitalizing on increasing AI investments and semiconductor demand. The article details their financial performance, growth prospects, and strong Zacks Ranks.

feed.title: Royal Bank of Canada (OTC: RBMCF) offers Barrier Digital Notes due 2031
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada (RBMCF) is offering Dual Directional Barrier Digital Notes due April 30, 2031, linked to the performance of the MSCI Emerging Markets Index and the EURO STOXX 50® Index. These notes provide an enhanced return potential with a digital return of at least 60% if the least performing underlier is at or above its initial value, and a principal at risk feature if it falls below 70% of its initial value. The offering price is 100% of the $1,000 principal amount, with an underwriting discount of 3.50%.

feed.title: [424B2] ROYAL BANK OF CANADA Prospectus Supplement
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada is offering Auto-Callable Enhanced Return Dual Directional Barrier Notes linked to the performance of Microsoft and Snowflake common stock. The notes feature an automatic call with a minimum payment, enhanced return potential, and absolute value return, but also carry principal at risk if specific barrier values are breached. Key dates include Trade Date April 27, 2026, Issue Date April 30, 2026, Valuation Date April 27, 2029, and Maturity Date May 2, 2029, with all payments subject to the bank's credit risk.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Has Lowered Expectations for Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO) Stock Price
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has slightly lowered its price target for Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO) from $295 to $293, while maintaining an "overweight" rating, suggesting a significant upside. The stock is currently trading down, and MarketBeat reports a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with a target of $284.31 from analysts. Insider sales have occurred, but institutional investors hold a large portion of the company's shares.

feed.title: Truist Financial Lowers RLJ Lodging Trust (NYSE:RLJ) Price Target to $7.00
feed.summary: Truist Financial has reduced its price target for RLJ Lodging Trust (NYSE:RLJ) from $8.00 to $7.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential 3.5% downside. This follows RLJ's better-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue, with the company also providing FY2026 EPS guidance. Institutional investors hold a significant stake in the company, which currently trades near $7.26 with a high P/E ratio.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges REGENXBIO Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against REGENXBIO Inc. (NASDAQ: RGNX) and its officers, alleging violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims that REGENXBIO made materially false and misleading statements regarding its RGX‑111 gene therapy for Hurler syndrome. Investors who purchased REGENXBIO securities between February 9, 2022, and January 27, 2026, are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 14, 2026.

feed.title: Gibraltar Industries (ROCK) posts 2025 results, outlines executive pay and 2026 votes
feed.summary: Gibraltar Industries (ROCK) has released its 2025 financial results, reporting approximately $1.1 billion in net sales, $3.25 GAAP EPS, $3.92 adjusted EPS, and an 18.3% return on invested capital. The company also announced details for its 2026 annual stockholders meeting, including votes on director elections, executive compensation, and auditor ratification. Executive compensation in 2025 saw CEO William T. Bosway receive over $4.7 million, with a significant portion linked to performance-based metrics, leading to a CEO-to-median-employee pay ratio of 63 to 1.

feed.title: Burger King, Taco Bell and the latest jobs report
feed.summary: Burger King aims to surpass McDonald's as the top burger chain, citing operational improvements and menu differentiation. Taco Bell is launching India's Butter Chicken Taco in the U.S. after a successful Global Taco Vote, catering to consumer demand for global fusion. Meanwhile, the foodservice industry added 21,500 jobs in March, recovering some February losses, but net job gains for the first quarter remain significantly lower than the previous year.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges uniQure N.V. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against uniQure N.V. (NASDAQ: QURE) and its officers, alleging violations of federal securities laws for investors who purchased securities between September 24, 2025, and October 31, 2025. The lawsuit claims uniQure made false and misleading statements regarding the FDA's approval of its Pivotal Study design and the likelihood of delaying its BLA timeline. Investors are encouraged to join the case, with an April 13, 2026 deadline to request lead plaintiff status.

feed.title: Albemarle Corp (ALB) Stock Price Down 3.79% on Apr 6
feed.summary: Shares of Albemarle Corp (ALB) fell 3.79% in mid-day trading on April 6th, reaching an intraday low of $170.80 before settling at $171.34. Wall Street analysts have an average target price of $190.66 for ALB, suggesting an 11.28% upside, and a consensus recommendation of "Outperform." However, GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $84.11, indicating a potential downside of 50.91%.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical Co (EMN) Trading Down 3.04% on Apr 6
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Co (EMN) shares dropped 3.04% to $72.79 on April 6th, placing it below its 52-week high but above its 52-week low. Analysts have an average price target of $77.62, implying a 6.63% upside, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $80.26, suggesting a 10.26% upside. The stock currently holds an "Outperform" consensus recommendation from brokerage firms.

feed.title: Kevin Hern makes significant sales in Accenture, Texas Instruments, and SmartStop Self Storage stocks
feed.summary: Kevin Hern, representative for Oklahoma’s 1st congressional district, through his family trusts, made significant stock sales in March 2026. These transactions included Accenture plc, Texas Instruments Incorporated, and SmartStop Self Storage REIT, Inc., with values ranging from $250,001 to $1,000,000 for these larger sales. He also sold stocks in several other companies like Becton, Dickinson and Company and Medtronic plc, with smaller transaction values, as recorded in a recent congressional trade report.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Enphase Energy, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Enphase overstated its ability to manage channel inventory and mitigate the impact of the 25D Credit termination, therefore misrepresenting its financial prospects. Investors are urged to join the case by April 20, 2026, to request lead plaintiff appointment.

feed.title: KKR to Buy 700-Unit Nothing Bundt Cakes for $2 Billion
feed.summary: KKR is set to acquire Nothing Bundt Cakes from Roark Capital for approximately $2 billion. This acquisition follows significant growth for Nothing Bundt Cakes under Roark Capital's ownership, expanding to 700 locations by May 2025. Roark Capital, a major player in restaurant franchises, also owns Inspire Brands and is reportedly exploring an IPO for it this year.

feed.title: Costco Wholesale Corporation stock: Q1 beat signals strength amid high valuation
feed.summary: Costco (COST) recently reported a strong Q1 CY2026 earnings beat, with revenue of $69.6 billion and EPS of $4.58, exceeding analyst expectations. The company's membership model continues to drive robust sales and customer loyalty, reinforcing its position as a defensive retail powerhouse. Despite a high valuation with a forward P/E of 46x, analysts maintain a positive outlook, citing Costco's consistent growth, international expansion, and strong competitive edge.

feed.title: Phillips 66: Wartime Beneficiary Of Surging Diesel And Chemicals Margin (Double Rating Upgrade)
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) has been upgraded to Strong Buy due to surging diesel and chemical margins, primarily driven by the war on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The company's heavy crude refining capacity and recent acquisition position it to benefit from high diesel prices and discounted Canadian heavy feedstock. Additionally, disruptions in Asian chemical supply are boosting CPChem's U.S. operations, and PSX's NGLs fractionation and LPG exports are highly leveraged to higher oil prices.

feed.title: Garmin Ltd. stock: Why fitness and aviation tech drives investor interest
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. (CH0114405324) is attracting investor interest due to its dual strength in consumer fitness wearables and critical aviation navigation systems. The company offers a blend of reliable tech innovation and dividend stability, with recent quarterly payouts of USD 1.05 per share, making it a compelling option for investors seeking growth and income without extreme volatility. Its diversified segments across fitness, outdoor, aviation, marine, and auto, along with its vertical integration and strong brand loyalty, provide a competitive edge in various markets.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Enphase Energy,
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) alleging securities law violations. The lawsuit claims Enphase overstated its ability to manage inventory and mitigate the effects of the 25D Credit termination, thus misrepresenting its financial prospects. Investors are encouraged to join the lawsuit by April 20, 2026, to seek recovery for losses incurred between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts S&P Global Price Target to $522 From $498, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for S&P Global (SPGI) to $522 from $498, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. S&P Global is a leading provider of financial information services, with key activities in commodities/energy/mobility information, financial ratings, and global stock market indices. The company's net sales are primarily generated in the United States, followed by Europe and Asia.
